perturbation.original_text: The Recipient, and its Representatives who have received Confidential Information pursuant hereto, shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose. The Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient, and its Representatives who have received sensitive data pursuant hereto, shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose. The Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'sensitive data' creates confusion because 'sensitive data' is not defined within the contract. This raises questions about whether sensitive data is broader or narrower than Confidential Information and whether there are different obligations associated with 'sensitive data' versus 'Confidential Information'. This creates uncertainty in enforcement as it's unclear which standard applies to which data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use and Disclosure of Confidential Information . The Recipient, and its Representatives who have received sensitive data pursuant hereto, shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose. The Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. The Recipient and such Representatives shall hold the Confidential Information in confidence, and provide it with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances, and shall not disclose any Confidential Information, except as permitted by paragraph 1 hereof or where such disclosure is requested or required by law, regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any organized securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of an entity’s securities are listed or quoted), regulatory body, judicial process, or listing agreement (collectively, “ Law ”). The Recipient agrees, to the extent permitted under applicable Law, to give the Disclosing Party notice of any such request or requirement as soon as reasonably practicable so that the Disclosing Party may, at the Disclosing Party’s own expense, seek a protective order, confidential treatment request or other appropriate remedy, and the Recipient shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to assist the Disclosing Party in obtaining such order or remedy. If, in the absence of a protective order, the Recipient is nonetheless compelled to disclose Confidential Information, the Recipient may disclose without liability hereunder that portion of the Confidential Information which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'sensitive data,' which is not defined in the contract. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information' and applies specific obligations to it. Because 'sensitive data' is undefined, it creates ambiguity as to whether the obligations related to Confidential Information also apply to 'sensitive data,' creating a direct conflict and uncertainty in enforcement, thereby fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Business Secrets or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Business Secrets' creates ambiguity regarding what's covered. 'Business Secrets' is undefined, creating potential conflict. Is 'Business Secrets' the same as trade secrets, or all 'Confidential Information', or something else entirely? This confusion makes it difficult to determine what is and isn't owned, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Business Secrets or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Business Secrets,' while the contradicted text refers to both 'Confidential Information' and 'Business Secrets.' According to the perturbation explanation, this replacement creates ambiguity because 'Business Secrets' is undefined, leading to uncertainty about what is actually covered by the agreement. This directly contradicts the section on 'Ownership of Confidential Information,' as it's now unclear whether the clause applies to 'Business Secrets' as well. This satisfies the Misaligned Terminology definition, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient and its Representatives to the Confidential Information. The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient and its Representatives to the Protected Material. The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Material' in the first sentence of Section 6 introduces a contradiction. The Disclosing Party can terminate access to 'Protected Material,' but the obligation to return or destroy is still tied to 'Confidential Information.' This could be interpreted to mean that the Recipient only needs to return or destroy information originally designated as Confidential Information, even if more information has become 'Protected Material'. The undefined term 'Protected Material' could lead to disputes over what must be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return or Destruction of Confidential Information . The Disclosing Party may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient and its Representatives to the Protected Material. The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof). Any destruction of materials shall be confirmed by the Recipient in writing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient and its Representatives may each retain copies of the Confidential Information for compliance with applicable laws, rules or regulations, bona fide data retention policies or to establish its rights under this Agreement. Any Confidential Information that cannot be or is not returned or destroyed (such as oral Confidential Information) shall remain confidential, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Material' in the first sentence, but the obligation to return or destroy only applies to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text also refers to both 'Protected Material' and 'Confidential Information'. The explanation states that this discrepancy creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the Recipient must return/destroy information classified as 'Protected Material' but not originally 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as the terms are used interchangeably without a clear definition, leading to conflicting obligations regarding what must be returned or destroyed.
